description: Cell observers fire in registration order, on every notification (#lzdartobservercow). Three observers registered a, b, c MUST be invoked a, b, c — and again a, b, c on the next publish, so the order is a property of the contract and not of one run. The fourth observer is registered after a publish and slots in at the back; the fifth case removes `b` and re-registers the same callback, which MUST go to the back rather than back to its old position, because order tracks the registration sequence and not the callback. What breaks otherwise: a set- or map-backed observer collection has no order to preserve, and the failure is not 'a different order' but a *different order each notification* — lazily-go's runtime randomizes map iteration deliberately, so its observers fired in a fresh sequence every publish, and lazily-py's set reorders on rehash. Callers write observers whose side effects compose (a log line, a queue push, a paint), and those compose differently under a shuffled order whether or not the contract admits it. Assertions are on `observed_order`, the exact sequence of observer labels invoked; nothing here names how a binding stores the list. Known to fail today against lazily-py (unspecified order) and lazily-zig (swap-remove relocates the tail over a removed entry).

expect.note: the order is stable across notifications, not merely present once
removal closes the gap; the survivors keep their relative order
re-registering the same callback appends — order follows the registration sequence, not the callback
the == guard suppresses the notification entirely; observers are not a way around it
